title: scaphochlamys uniflora
intro: Scaphochlamys uniflora, also known as the 'One-flowered Scaphochlamys', is a species of flowering plant in the family Zingiberaceae. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, particularly in Malaysia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and improving digestion. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes are typically dried and ground into a powder, which can be made into tea or mixed with other herbs for medicinal purposes.
